headline: Asian Cities Dominate Latest Global Liveability Rankings
meta_description: The 2026 Global Liveability Index shows Asian cities climbing rankings, reflecting urban planning and quality-of-life improvements across the region.
summary_short: The latest Global Liveability Index reveals a significant rise in rankings for Asian cities, reflecting improvements in urban infrastructure, services, and quality of li…
body:
The most recent Global Liveability Index has drawn attention to a remarkable shift in how cities worldwide are being evaluated. Asian cities have climbed substantially in the rankings, signaling that urban centers across the region are delivering tangible improvements to residents' everyday lives. The index measures factors that directly affect how people experience their cities—from safety and infrastructure to healthcare and education.

This upward movement reflects years of investment in urban planning, transportation systems, and public services. Cities across Asia have prioritized creating environments where people can live, work, and raise families with confidence. Better connectivity, cleaner air initiatives, and expanded access to healthcare and education all contribute to higher liveability scores.

The rankings serve as both recognition of progress made and a benchmark for cities worldwide. As Asian urban centers continue to innovate in sustainable design and quality-of-life improvements, they offer valuable lessons for cities everywhere. The data demonstrates that intentional investment in livable communities yields measurable results that residents experience every day.
